title: Ischaemum byrone
intro: Ischaemum byrone, also known as Byrone grass, is a species of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms of fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Byrone grass include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Byrone grass include decoctions and infusions, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and skin infections.
taxonomy: Ischaemum byrone is classified as a species of grass in the family Poaceae. This classification places it within the subfamily Chloridoideae and the tribe Eragrostideae. Ischaemum byrone is further categorized as a member of the genus Ischaemum, a genus of grasses that are native to tropical and subtropical regions. The plant's classification indicates its close relationship to other grasses in the same family and subfamily.
